# RoboCraft BR — Context for LLMs

## Overview
Guia completo para montar robôs com IA no Brasil. Inclui projetos com peças do Mercado Livre, TTS (text-to-speech) local e na nuvem, e integração com DimOS + Modal GLM-5.1.

## Site Structure
- / (index) — Step-by-step selection guide
- /wiki — Full documentation wiki
- /llms.txt — This file

## Projects (5 available)

### 1. ESP32-CAM Visão Computacional
- ID: esp32-cam
- Cost: R$ 35–55
- Difficulty: Easy
- Time: 1–2 hours
- Use cases: Security camera, plant monitoring, computer vision, IoT
- Parts: ESP32-CAM AI-Thinker, FTDI module, MicroSD 8GB, Case 3D

### 2. Cabeça Humanóide Falante
- ID: head-humanoid
- Cost: R$ 150–400
- Difficulty: Easy
- Time: 4–6 hours
- Use cases: Buddhist temples, museums, reception, interactive presentations
- Parts: Kit Olhos Animatrônicos, Arduino Uno R3, PCA9685 16ch, Servos SG90(4x), DFPlayer Mini, Alto-falante 3W, MicroSD 8GB, Fonte 5V 2A

### 3. Robô Braitenberg V2
- ID: braitenberg
- Cost: R$ 80–150
- Difficulty: Easy
- Time: 2–3 hours
- Use cases: Education, science fairs, basic competitions
- Parts: Chassi 2WD, Arduino Uno, L298N Driver, LDR(2x), Bateria 9V
- Note: Reactive robot that follows light sources automatically

### 4. Carro 2WD com Câmera
- ID: carro-2wd
- Cost: R$ 200–350
- Difficulty: Medium
- Time: 6–8 hours
- Use cases: Patrolling, remote monitoring, STEM education, competitions
- Parts: Chassi 2WD, ESP32-CAM, FTDI module, L298N Driver, Bateria 18650(2x), HC-SR04 Ultrasonic, Jumpers

### 5. Braço Robótico 4DOF
- ID: braco-servo
- Cost: R$ 200–350
- Difficulty: Hard
- Time: 8–12 hours
- Use cases: Pick and place, education, research, competitions
- Parts: Servos MG996R(4x), Arduino Mega, PCA9685 Driver, Joystick PS2, Acrylic structure, Fonte 5V 3A

## TTS Providers (8 available)

### Cloud TTS
- Edge TTS: Free, High quality, Low latency, pt-BR excellent, voices: pt-BR-FranciscaNeural
- gTTS: Free, Medium quality, Medium latency, Simple Google Translate API
- ElevenLabs: Free (10k chars/month), Ultra High quality, Voice cloning available
- OpenAI TTS: Paid ($15/1M chars), High quality, Low latency, voices: alloy, echo, fable, nova

### Local TTS (no internet required)
- Piper TTS: Free, High quality, <50ms latency, Runs on Raspberry Pi, model: pt_BR-edresson
- Coqui TTS: Free, Very High quality, Medium latency, Supports voice training
- Mimic3 TTS: Free, High quality, Medium latency, Mozilla-based
- eSpeak NG: Free, Low (robotic) quality, Instant, Runs on any MCU including Arduino/ESP32

## Shops (6 recommended)
1. Mercado Livre — Marketplace, 4.8 stars, Everything
2. RoboCore — Specialized, 4.7 stars, Educational content
3. FilipeFlop — Specialized, 4.6 stars, Free shipping R$150
4. Shopee Brasil — Marketplace, 4.3 stars, Lower prices
5. Santa Ifigênia — Physical store (São Paulo), 4.5 stars, Test before buying
6. Amazon Brasil — Marketplace, 4.7 stars, Prime delivery

## Technical Information

### Arduino Pinouts
- A4: I2C SDA
- A5: I2C SCL
- D0-D13: Digital GPIO
- D3, D5, D6, D9, D10, D11: PWM
- A0-A5: ADC

### ESP32-CAM Pinouts
- U0T: TX (FTDI RX)
- U0R: RX (FTDI TX)
- GPIO0: Flash mode (connect to GND to program)
- EN: Enable (internal pull-up)

### Libraries Needed
- Adafruit PWM Servo Driver Library
- DFPlayer Mini MP3
- Servo
- NewPing (HC-SR04)
- Wire (I2C)

## DimOS Integration
- Platform: dimos.agents.modal_agent
- Model: Modal GLM-5.1-FP8
- API: OpenAI-compatible
- Install: pip install uv && uv pip install dimos
- Configure: MODAL_API_KEY in .env

## Step-by-Step Flow
1. Choose robot project (/#robots)
2. View parts list (BOM) and pinouts
3. Select TTS voice (/#tts)
4. Choose shops to buy parts
5. Follow assembly guide (/#guides)
6. Set up DimOS integration (/#dimos)

## Links
- DimOS: https://github.com/dimensionalOS/dimos
- Modal: https://modal.com
- Piper TTS: https://github.com/rhasspy/piper
- Coqui TTS: https://github.com/coqui-ai/TTS
- Edge TTS: https://github.com/rany2/edge-tts
- ElevenLabs: https://elevenlabs.io/

## Tech Stack
- Frontend: Vue 3 (CDN)
- Fonts: IBM Plex Sans, Space Mono, JetBrains Mono
- Hosting: GitHub Pages
- Theme: Dark AMOLED (#050505) with squared red (#ff3333) / green (#00cc44) buttons, no gradients, no AI slop aesthetics
- Responsive: Mobile-first design

## Hardware Requirements by Project
- ESP32-CAM: FTDI programmer, MicroSD card
- Cabeça Humanóide: Arduino IDE, libraries
- Carro 2WD: Wi-Fi network 2.4GHz
- Braço Robótico: External power supply 5V 3A

## Common Issues
- ESP32-CAM not connecting: Check GPIO0-GND connection during flash
- Servos not responding: Verify 60Hz PWM frequency, angles 150-600
- DFPlayer not playing: Files must be named 001.mp3, 002.mp3, etc.
- Wi-Fi issues: Use 2.4GHz network (not 5GHz)
